members.name: Align
members.summary: Aligns the camera to the tracked node. [b]Note:[/b] Calling [method force_update_scroll] after this method is not required.

members.name: ForceUpdateScroll
members.summary: Forces the camera to update scroll immediately.

members.name: GetDragMargin
members.summary: Returns the specified [enum Side]'s margin. See also [member drag_bottom_margin], [member drag_top_margin], [member drag_left_margin], and [member drag_right_margin].

members.name: GetLimit
members.summary: Returns the camera limit for the specified [enum Side]. See also [member limit_bottom], [member limit_top], [member limit_left], and [member limit_right].

members.name: GetScreenCenterPosition
members.summary: Returns the center of the screen from this camera's point of view, in global coordinates. [b]Note:[/b] The exact targeted position of the camera may be different. See [method get_target_position].

members.name: GetScreenRotation
members.summary: Returns the current screen rotation from this camera's point of view. [b]Note:[/b] The screen rotation can be different from [member Node2D.global_rotation] if the camera is rotating smoothly due to [member rotation_smoothing_enabled].

members.name: GetTargetPosition
members.summary: Returns this camera's target position, in global coordinates. [b]Note:[/b] The returned value is not the same as [member Node2D.global_position], as it is affected by the drag properties. It is also not the same as the current position if [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code] (see [method get_screen_center_position]).

members.name: IsCurrent
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Camera2D] is the active camera (see [method Viewport.get_camera_2d]).

members.name: MakeCurrent
members.summary: Forces this [Camera2D] to become the current active one. [member enabled] must be [code]true[/code].

members.name: ResetSmoothing
members.summary: Sets the camera's position immediately to its current smoothing destination. This method has no effect if [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]false[/code].

members.name: SetDragMargin
members.signature: public void SetDragMargin(int margin, float dragMargin)
members.summary: Sets the specified [enum Side]'s margin. See also [member drag_bottom_margin], [member drag_top_margin], [member drag_left_margin], and [member drag_right_margin].

members.name: SetLimit
members.signature: public void SetLimit(int margin, int limit)
members.summary: Sets the camera limit for the specified [enum Side]. See also [member limit_bottom], [member limit_top], [member limit_left], and [member limit_right].

members.name: AnchorMode
members.summary: The Camera2D's anchor point.

members.name: CustomViewport
members.summary: The custom [Viewport] node attached to the [Camera2D]. If [code]null[/code] or not a [Viewport], uses the default viewport instead.

members.name: DragBottomMargin
members.summary: Bottom margin needed to drag the camera. A value of [code]1[/code] makes the camera move only when reaching the bottom edge of the screen.

members.name: DragHorizontalEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the camera only moves when reaching the horizontal (left and right) drag margins. If [code]false[/code], the camera moves horizontally regardless of margins.

members.name: DragHorizontalOffset
members.summary: The relative horizontal drag offset of the camera between the right ([code]-1[/code]) and left ([code]1[/code]) drag margins. [b]Note:[/b] Used to set the initial horizontal drag offset; determine the current offset; or force the current offset. It's not automatically updated when [member drag_horizontal_enabled] is [code]true[/code] or the drag margins are changed.

members.name: DragLeftMargin
members.summary: Left margin needed to drag the camera. A value of [code]1[/code] makes the camera move only when reaching the left edge of the screen.

members.name: DragRightMargin
members.summary: Right margin needed to drag the camera. A value of [code]1[/code] makes the camera move only when reaching the right edge of the screen.

members.name: DragTopMargin
members.summary: Top margin needed to drag the camera. A value of [code]1[/code] makes the camera move only when reaching the top edge of the screen.

members.name: DragVerticalEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the camera only moves when reaching the vertical (top and bottom) drag margins. If [code]false[/code], the camera moves vertically regardless of the drag margins.

members.name: DragVerticalOffset
members.summary: The relative vertical drag offset of the camera between the bottom ([code]-1[/code]) and top ([code]1[/code]) drag margins. [b]Note:[/b] Used to set the initial vertical drag offset; determine the current offset; or force the current offset. It's not automatically updated when [member drag_vertical_enabled] is [code]true[/code] or the drag margins are changed.

members.name: EditorDrawDragMargin
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], draws the camera's drag margin rectangle in the editor.

members.name: EditorDrawLimits
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], draws the camera's limits rectangle in the editor.

members.name: EditorDrawScreen
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], draws the camera's screen rectangle in the editor.

members.name: Enabled
members.summary: Controls whether the camera can be active or not. If [code]true[/code], the [Camera2D] will become the main camera when it enters the scene tree and there is no active camera currently (see [method Viewport.get_camera_2d]). When the camera is currently active and [member enabled] is set to [code]false[/code], the next enabled [Camera2D] in the scene tree will become active.

members.name: IgnoreRotation
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the camera's rendered view is not affected by its [member Node2D.rotation] and [member Node2D.global_rotation].

members.name: LimitBottom
members.summary: Bottom scroll limit in pixels. The camera stops moving when reaching this value, but [member offset] can push the view past the limit.

members.name: LimitEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the limits will be enabled. Disabling this will allow the camera to focus anywhere, when the four [code]limit_*[/code] properties will not work.

members.name: LimitLeft
members.summary: Left scroll limit in pixels. The camera stops moving when reaching this value, but [member offset] can push the view past the limit.

members.name: LimitRight
members.summary: Right scroll limit in pixels. The camera stops moving when reaching this value, but [member offset] can push the view past the limit.

members.name: LimitSmoothed
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the camera smoothly stops when reaches its limits. This property has no effect if [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]false[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] To immediately update the camera's position to be within limits without smoothing, even with this setting enabled, invoke [method reset_smoothing].

members.name: LimitTop
members.summary: Top scroll limit in pixels. The camera stops moving when reaching this value, but [member offset] can push the view past the limit.

members.name: Offset
members.summary: The camera's relative offset. Useful for looking around or camera shake animations. The offsetted camera can go past the limits defined in [member limit_top], [member limit_bottom], [member limit_left] and [member limit_right].

members.name: PositionSmoothingEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the camera's view smoothly moves towards its target position at [member position_smoothing_speed].

members.name: PositionSmoothingSpeed
members.summary: Speed in pixels per second of the camera's smoothing effect when [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: ProcessCallback
members.summary: The camera's process callback.

members.name: RotationSmoothingEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the camera's view smoothly rotates, via asymptotic smoothing, to align with its target rotation at [member rotation_smoothing_speed]. [b]Note:[/b] This property has no effect if [member ignore_rotation] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: RotationSmoothingSpeed
members.summary: The angular, asymptotic speed of the camera's rotation smoothing effect when [member rotation_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: Zoom
members.summary: The camera's zoom. Higher values are more zoomed in. For example, a zoom of [code]Vector2(2.0, 2.0)[/code] will be twice as zoomed in on each axis (the view covers an area four times smaller). In contrast, a zoom of [code]Vector2(0.5, 0.5)[/code] will be twice as zoomed out on each axis (the view covers an area four times larger). The X and Y components should generally always be set to the same value, unless you wish to stretch the camera view. [b]Note:[/b] [member FontFile.oversampling] does [i]not[/i] take [Camera2D] zoom into account. This means that zooming in/out will cause bitmap fonts and rasterized (non-MSDF) dynamic fonts to appear blurry or pixelated unless the font is part of a [CanvasLayer] that makes it ignore camera zoom. To ensure text remains crisp regardless of zoom, you can enable MSDF font rendering by enabling [member ProjectSettings.gui/theme/default_font_multichannel_signed_distance_field] (applies to the default project font only), or enabling [b]Multichannel Signed Distance Field[/b] in the import options of a DynamicFont for custom fonts. On system fonts, [member SystemFont.multichannel_signed_distance_field] can be enabled in the inspector.

values.name: ANCHOR_MODE_DRAG_CENTER
values.summary: The camera's position takes into account vertical/horizontal offsets and the screen size.

values.name: ANCHOR_MODE_FIXED_TOP_LEFT
values.summary: The camera's position is fixed so that the top-left corner is always at the origin.

values.name: CAMERA2D_PROCESS_IDLE
values.summary: The camera updates during process frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PROCESS]).

values.name: CAMERA2D_PROCESS_PHYSICS
values.summary: The camera updates during physics frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PHYSICS_PROCESS]).

constants.name: ANCHOR_MODE_FIXED_TOP_LEFT
constants.summary: The camera's position is fixed so that the top-left corner is always at the origin.

constants.name: ANCHOR_MODE_DRAG_CENTER
constants.summary: The camera's position takes into account vertical/horizontal offsets and the screen size.

constants.name: CAMERA2D_PROCESS_PHYSICS
constants.summary: The camera updates during physics frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PHYSICS_PROCESS]).

constants.name: CAMERA2D_PROCESS_IDLE
constants.summary: The camera updates during process frames (see [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_PROCESS]).
